name: read_tickets_tool
summary: Read tickets from the queue: look up by key, list by status or label, or search task bodies.
Returns a markdown ticket block (`get`) or a bulleted summary list (`list`, `search`).
constraints: `get` defaults `key` to the agent's current ticket — omit `key` to read your own work.
`list` and `search` cap at 50 tickets per call; tighten filters rather than re-running.
anti_patterns: To create or edit tickets — use `manage_tickets_tool`.
When the agent needs read AND write access in the same flow — use `manage_tickets_tool` instead of registering both.
To find code or files in the working directory — use `grep_tool` / `glob_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: `search` matches the task body only — labels and result text are not searched.
output: `get`: a markdown block with key, status, reporter, labels, task body, and result.

action.description: Read mode: `get` (one ticket), `list` (filter by status / label), `search` (free-text).

key.description: Ticket key for `get` (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Defaults to the agent's current ticket. Ignored by `list` / `search`.

status.description: Filter for `list`: one of `Todo`, `InProgress`, `Finished`, `Failed`. Combine with `label` to narrow further.

label.description: Filter for `list`: only tickets carrying this label (case-sensitive). Pass an agent's name to find that agent's pinned tickets.

query.description: Free-text query for `search`. Case-insensitive substring match against the task body.
